prompt: The system processes incoming HTTP requests through a layered middleware stack. Each layer is responsible for a specific concern: request ID generation happens in the outermost middleware, followed by structured logging, then request body size limits, then rate limiting per IP, then authentication token validation, then per-route authorization checks, and finally the route handler itself executes. Each middleware layer MUST propagate the request_id through the context, and any error returned from a layer must include the request_id in its structured log entry. Failure to do so will break trace correlation in our observability stack. In production, we have observed that the rate limiter layer can become a bottleneck under DDoS conditions, and we mitigated this by moving from an in-memory token bucket to a Redis-backed sliding window counter. The Redis key structure is rate:limit:{ip}:{window} with a 60-second TTL. The trade-off was a small additional latency per request (typically under 1ms) in exchange for accurate distributed rate limiting across multiple API replicas. This change shipped in CHG-0823 on 2026-05-14 and has been stable in production for 60+ days. The system processes incoming HTTP requests through a layered middleware stack.
